sections.content: Feature flags are often treated as release toggles, but they are much more powerful. In production, they allow instant rollback without redeploy, gradual rollouts, A/B testing, and emergency shutdown of risky features.
When a feature causes issues, you can disable the flag, contain the damage, and investigate safely. Without flags, rollback requires redeployment under pressure.
Feature flags reduce blast radius. Every high-risk feature should be behind one. They turn deployments into reversible decisions.
